title: Phenol/Chloroform Genomic DNA Extraction from Tissue Culture Cells
author: John Tyson
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7ejhjcn

steps.title: Remove tissue culture media from two...
steps.instruction: Remove tissue culture media from two T25 flasks of cells ( ~ 1 x 10 7 cells / per flask, or 60 – 80 % confluency)

steps.title: Pour the viscous sample into a...
steps.instruction: Pour the viscous sample into a 50 ml tube after gently scrapping the surface of the two T25’s with a cell scrapper.

steps.title: Incubate for 01:00:00 at 37 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate for 01:00:00 at 37 °C .

steps.title: Add proteinase K (20mg/ml) to a...
steps.instruction: Add proteinase K (20mg/ml) to a final concentration of 100 μg/ml .

steps.title: Incubate the lysate at 56 °C for 03:00:00
steps.instruction: Incubate the lysate at 56 °C for 03:00:00 . (occasionally mix by inversion).

steps.title: Allow to cool to Room temperature...
steps.instruction: Allow to cool to Room temperature before adding an equal volume of buffer-saturated phenol.

steps.title: Gently mix by inversion for a...
steps.instruction: Gently mix by inversion for a few minutes until an emulsion is formed.

steps.title: Separate the two phases by centrifugation...
steps.instruction: Separate the two phases by centrifugation at 5000 x g for 00:30:00 – 01:00:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Use a wide-bore pipette to transfer...
steps.instruction: Use a wide-bore pipette to transfer the upper viscous aqueous phase to a fresh tube being careful not to disturb any material at the interface. Further Phenol extraction steps may be required if there is a large quantity of material at the interface i.e. when performing extractions from tissue samples.

steps.title: Add an equal volume of Chloroform...
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume of Chloroform and mix by gentle inversion for a minute or so.

steps.title: Separate the two phases by centrifugation...
steps.instruction: Separate the two phases by centrifugation at 5000 x g for 00:30:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Use a wide-bore pipette to transfer...
steps.instruction: Use a wide-bore pipette to transfer the upper viscous aqueous phase to a fresh 50 ml tube being careful not to disturb the interface.

steps.title: Add NaCl to increase concentration by 0
steps.instruction: Add NaCl to increase concentration by 0.3 Molarity (M) from a 5 Molarity (M) stock.

steps.title: Add 2 volumes of ethanol
steps.instruction: Add 2 volumes of ethanol.

steps.title: Wash the DNA condensate by dipping...
steps.instruction: Wash the DNA condensate by dipping into a series of three 1 ml tubes containing 70 % ethanol and holding there for ~ 00:00:20 .

steps.title: Allow the DNA on the glass...
steps.instruction: Allow the DNA on the glass capillary to air-dry for ~ 00:01:00 (hold upside down and allow any excess liquid to rundown and dab away with a tissue avoiding the DNA).

steps.title: Place the DNA on the end...
steps.instruction: Place the DNA on the end of the glass capillary into a tube containing 100 µL – 200 µL Buffer EB ( 10 Molarity (M) Tris-Cl pH 8.0).

steps.title: Gently dislodge the DNA from the glass capillary
steps.instruction: Gently dislodge the DNA from the glass capillary. You should be left with a viscous solution with no “gel” mass on the glass capillary.

steps.title: Place the DNA solution in the...
steps.instruction: Place the DNA solution in the fridge and leave for a few days to fully hydrate before use. We generally see a yield of 200 – 400 μg of genomic DNA. High viscosity is a good indication of HMW DNA being present (using a pipette draw a small volume up and then pull out from the surface of the DNA solution. If you see a continuous strand connecting the tip and solution you are good). It can take a while for HMW DNA solutions to “homogenise” when diluted due to viscosity and this often makes reliable quantification hard. We will often prepare 1/5 or other appropriate dilutions a day ahead of intended processing to help with this issue.

steps.title: Lyse cells by adding 5 mL...
steps.instruction: Lyse cells by adding 5 mL of Lysis buffer per flask and gentle rocking back and forth for ~ 00:01:00 .

steps.title: Mix by gentle inversion of the tube several times
steps.instruction: Mix by gentle inversion of the tube several times.

steps.title: Mix by gentle inversion
steps.instruction: Mix by gentle inversion.

steps.title: Mix by inversion a couple of...
steps.instruction: Mix by inversion a couple of times and then gentle swirling. You should see a stringy mass of DNA condense together and this is hooked out using a glass capillary with a hook made at the end.

steps.title: Let sit at Room temperature for ~ 00:30:00
steps.instruction: Let sit at Room temperature for ~ 00:30:00 .
